summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering several personnel matters, a nuisance property resolution, the school resource officers MOU renewal, and Memorial Day parade planning. The board accepted the resignation of Division Fire Chief Jake Rose and two probationary police officers, approved three new part-time firefighter/EMT hires, and designated all Purple Heart recipients in Goshen Township as grand marshals for the 2024 Memorial Day Parade. A nuisance abatement resolution was approved for a property at 7000 Willow Ridge Court that has been in violation for four years.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for April 9-23, 2024
Directed Administrator Rose to attend Sunshine Law certified training on behalf of elected officials
Approved out-of-town training for Jen Farmer for Tactical Tools for Thriving training at $50
Scheduled special Board of Trustees zoning workshop for May 13, 2024 at 6 PM
Accepted resignation of Division Fire Chief Jake Rose effective 5/7/24
Approved Resolution R-007-2024 declaring nuisance at 7000 Willow Ridge Court (Parcel 114343B038)
Approved $1,000 transfer from general fund to zoning for nuisance abatement
Approved $1,000 appropriation in zoning fund for property services nuisance abatement
Accepted $50 donation from Shannon and Esther Wilder to fire department
Accepted resignation of Officer Megan Sickles effective 4/10/24
Accepted resignation of Officer Daniel Pangallo effective 4/17/24
Approved MOU with Goshen Local Schools for school resource officers program for 2024-2025 school year
Selected all Purple Heart recipients in Goshen Township as 2024 Memorial Day Parade grand marshals
Adjourned into executive session at 6:28 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1)
Appointed Austin Taylor as part-time firefighter/EMT at $16/hour effective May 5, 2024
Appointed Austin Cannon as part-time firefighter/EMT at $16/hour effective May 5, 2024
Appointed Cameron Mueller as part-time firefighter/EMT at $16/hour effective May 5, 2024

$1,000 appropriated for nuisance abatement at 7000 Willow Ridge Court
$50 donation from Shannon and Esther Wilder to fire department
$16/hour pay rate for three new part-time firefighter/EMTs
Goshen Local Schools pays approximately 55% of salary and benefits for two SROs
$48 per car for magnetic Grand Marshal signs, $15-30 per car for banner signs
notable_topics: Nuisance property abatement at 7000 Willow Ridge Court (4-year ongoing case)
Resignation of Division Fire Chief Jake Rose
Resignation of two probationary police officers (Sickles and Pangallo)
School Resource Officer MOU renewal with Goshen Local Schools for 2024-2025
Memorial Day Parade planning - Purple Heart recipients as grand marshals
BZA hearing commendation letter from Jerry Henlon
attendees: Ray Otten (mentioned - potential Memorial Day speaker)
notes: Executive session lasted from 6:28 PM to 7:57 PM. Trustee Hodges shared history about overturning the township's nepotism rule years ago with Trustees Audrey and Jack Coons, which allowed Jake Rose to begin his fire service career. The nuisance property at 7000 Willow Ridge Court has been an open case for 4 years with no response from property owners despite multiple violation notices and a mediation hearing request. Previous zoning director did not follow through on reimbursement from a 2021 nuisance declaration. Mr. O (band director) confirmed for Memorial Day parade with Heisler kids marching.
title: BOT 4-23-24
